title: 1.3 — K8s Operator + CRDs
description: Kubebuilder v4 operator (cmd/operator/) inside the monorepo. CRDs: OCIRegistry (declarative registry registration), ScanRequest (one-shot scan via K8s Job using --once contract from ADR-027), APIKey (declarative API key written to K8s Secret). Reconcile loops call pkg/client (ocidex-3nu). API group: ocidex.io/v1alpha1. Blocked by ocidex-3nu (Go client SDK).

title: ADR-030: K8s Operator CRD schema design
description: Write docs/adr/0030-k8s-operator-crds.md. Cover: CRD names (OCIRegistry, ScanRequest, APIKey), API group (ocidex.io/v1alpha1), spec field mapping to API types, status condition types (Ready, Progressing, Degraded), idref for APIKey secretRef, ScanRequest phase states (Pending/Running/Succeeded/Failed), leader election scope, RBAC requirements.
notes:
Files: docs/adr/0030-k8s-operator-crds.md
Approach: Documented all three CRD specs (OCIRegistry, ScanRequest, APIKey) and reconcile contracts. Key decision: ScanRequest is fire-and-forget because ScanRegistryOutputBody only returns {message: string} — no job ID available. Phase transitions to Dispatched terminal state. APIKey uses generation-based rotation detection. All three CRDs are namespace-scoped.

title: kubebuilder scaffold: api/v1alpha1/, cmd/operator/, config/operator/
description: Run kubebuilder init + kubebuilder create api for OCIRegistry, ScanRequest, APIKey CRDs. Output: api/v1alpha1/ with types and groupversion_info.go, cmd/operator/main.go with manager setup, config/operator/ with generated RBAC and CRD install manifests. No reconcile logic yet. Depends on ADR-030.
notes:
Files: api/v1alpha1/ (groupversion_info.go, ociregistry_types.go, scanrequest_types.go, apikey_types.go, zz_generated.deepcopy.go, zz_generated.deepcopy_extra.go), cmd/operator/main.go, config/operator/crd/ (3 CRD YAMLs), Makefile (generate-operator + build operator), CLAUDE.md (beads+repomix workflow, controller-gen docs, golangci-lint v2, project structure)
Approach: Hand-written kubebuilder v4 scaffold; controller-gen v0.21.0 generates deepcopy/CRD manifests. Added zz_generated.deepcopy_extra.go to supply DeepCopyInto for Spec/Status types (controller-gen v0.21.0 generates calls but not implementations for sub-types). go.mod bumped to 1.26.0 (required by controller-runtime v0.24.1). Scheme registration uses runtime.NewSchemeBuilder (scheme.Builder is deprecated).

title: OCIRegistry CRD types + controller
description: Define OCIRegistrySpec (mirrors CreateRegistryRequest fields) and OCIRegistryStatus (id string, conditions []metav1.Condition). Implement reconcile loop: fetch CRD, call client.GetRegistry or CreateRegistry/UpdateRegistry as needed, set Ready condition, store registry ID in status. Handle deletion via finalizer: call DeleteRegistry before removing finalizer.
notes:
Files: internal/controller/ociregistry_controller.go, internal/controller/conditions.go, internal/controller/ociregistry_controller_test.go, cmd/operator/main.go
Approach: OCIRegistryReconciler with create/update/delete+finalizer pattern. GenerationChangedPredicate prevents status-write re-reconcile loops; finalizer-add + re-fetch continues in same call to work around predicate filtering. handleDeletion extracted to keep Reconcile cc<15. SetCondition exported to conditions.go for reuse by 01v.4/5. 7 table-driven tests using controller-runtime fake client + FakeClient.

title: ScanRequest CRD types + controller
description: Define ScanRequestSpec (registryRef corev1.LocalObjectReference, optional repository/digest/tag strings) and ScanRequestStatus (phase string, jobID string, sbomID string, conditions). Reconcile: look up OCIRegistry ID from status, call ScanRegistry via SDK; status tracks returned job ID; poll job state until terminal. One-shot: set status.phase=Succeeded and do not re-reconcile.
notes:
Files: internal/controller/scanrequest_controller.go, internal/controller/scanrequest_controller_test.go, cmd/operator/main.go
Approach: Fire-and-forget; phase guard (Dispatched|Failed → no-op) prevents re-dispatch; cross-namespace OCIRegistry lookup reads status.registryID; no finalizer; RequeueAfter 10s when registry not ready; phaseDispatched/Failed/Pending constants. 6 tests.

title: APIKey CRD types + controller
description: Define APIKeySpec (name string, scope string, secretRef corev1.LocalObjectReference) and APIKeyStatus (keyID string, prefix string, conditions). Reconcile: if key not created, call CreateAPIKey, write plaintext to secretRef Secret; if key exists (status.keyID set), verify it still exists via ListAPIKeys; if CRD deleted, call DeleteAPIKey then remove finalizer. Rotation: detect spec change, delete old + create new key, update secret.
notes:
Files: internal/controller/apikey_controller.go, internal/controller/apikey_controller_test.go, cmd/operator/main.go
Approach: APIKeyReconciler with create/verify/rotate/delete+finalizer. CreateAPIKey returns no ID so list-after-create matches new key by prefix[:8]. Rotation detected via ready.ObservedGeneration < cr.Generation. Secret written via controllerutil.CreateOrUpdate. handleDeletion deletes key + Secret + finalizer. 7 tests: create, verify-no-rotation, re-create-on-missing, rotation, delete, delete-ignores-404, API error.

title: Package view: tree-first layout with rich node detail
description:
The SBOM detail page currently has two separate tabs — a flat table (Packages) and a basic tree (Dependency Tree). The tree shows only name + dep-count badge; the flat table has version, type badge, and PURL. Users lose all the useful column detail when switching to tree view.

**What needs to change:**
Collapse the two tabs into a single view. The primary layout should be the dependency tree (collapsed by default), but each node must carry the same details currently shown in the flat table: version, ecosystem/type badge, and PURL. A toggle in the toolbar should let users switch to the classic flat list.

**Specific design:**
- Tree mode (default): roots shown collapsed. Each row shows name (linked), version (mono), type badge, dep-count badge. Expanding a node reveals its direct deps as indented rows with the same detail columns.
- Flat mode: existing PackagesTab table, unchanged.
- Toggle: a small segmented control or icon button (Tree | List) in the filter bar.
- The DependencyTreeView component currently renders in a fixed 600px max-height div with no columns — replace that row-by-row approach with a proper table layout where the indent/expand toggle occupies the Name column.

**Affected files:**
- web/src/pages/SBOMDetail/PackagesTab.tsx — DependencyTreeView, PackagesTab, TreeNodeRow
- web/src/pages/SBOMDetail/index.tsx — remove separate Dependencies tab, pass combined view

**Out of scope:** diff/compare pages (covered by a separate issue)
notes:
Files: web/src/pages/SBOMDetail/PackagesTab.tsx, web/src/pages/SBOMDetail/index.tsx
Approach: Collapsed Packages+Dependencies tabs into a single view. PackagesTab now accepts optional depsGraph prop; when deps data with edges is available, Tree/List toggle appears in the filter bar and tree is shown by default (roots collapsed). DependencyTreeView rebuilt as a proper <table> with Name/Version/Type/Deps columns. TreeNodeRow renders <tr> elements with depth-based padding in the Name cell. Deps always eagerly loaded; tab signal and tab-bar removed from index.tsx.
close_reason: Collapsed Packages+Dependencies tabs into unified tree-first view with Tree/List toggle

title: Spike: tree view animation and expand-all without browser crash
description:
## Why this exists

Two attempts to add smooth animation and expand-all to the dependency tree view (ocidex-0fp) both caused browser crashes. This spike should produce a concrete, validated approach before any implementation.

## What was tried

### Attempt 1 — always-mount all descendants
Pre-render the entire tree in the DOM (collapsed via CSS) so expand/collapse can animate without mount/unmount latency. Used CSS grid-template-rows: 0fr/1fr transition.

**Result:** Crashed immediately. A 500-package SBOM creates 500+ TreeNodeRow components each with createSignal + createMemo + createEffect + a recursive Set copy (nextVisited). The browser tab ran out of memory before painting anything.

### Attempt 2 — lazy-mount + cascade expand-all via createEffect(on(...))
Kept <Show when={expanded()}> for lazy mount. Added a SolidJS context carrying collapse/expand counter signals. Each TreeNodeRow ran createEffect(on(ctx.expand, ...)) without defer so newly-mounted children would also expand immediately — cascading depth-first until all nodes were expanded.

**Result:** Expand All still crashed. Rapidly mounting hundreds of components in a synchronous reactive cascade overwhelms the browser even with lazy mount. Collapse All worked fine (just sets expanded=false on mounted nodes).

## Root causes identified

1. **Reactive component count scales with O(visible nodes × depth)** — each TreeNodeRow allocates several reactive primitives; for large SBOMs even the lazy-mount approach can mount too many in one synchronous batch.

2. **nextVisited memo is O(depth) per node** — creates a new Set at every depth level, copying all ancestors. For a flat tree of 500 nodes each at depth 1 this is cheap, but real package DAGs can have shared nodes reached via long paths.

3. **Expand-all cascade is unbounded** — triggering a reactive signal that causes N mounts, each immediately running an effect that causes more mounts, is a synchronous avalanche.

## What to research

- **Centralized expansion state**: lift the Set<nodeRef> of expanded nodes to the tree root instead of per-node signals. Expand All becomes a single Set.add of all refs with no reactivity cascade. TreeNodeRow reads from this set reactively.

- **Virtualization**: render only the visible rows (windowed/virtual list). AG Grid, TanStack Virtual, or a simpler custom approach. Each row is a fixed-height div; only rows in the viewport are mounted.

- **Expand-all without cascade**: if expansion state is centralized, expand-all can be a single batch: compute all reachable non-cyclic refs, fill the Set, re-render. Children are still lazy but their "should expand" state is already in the Set when they mount.

- **Animation without always-mount**: CSS @starting-style (Chrome 117+, Firefox 129+) allows entry transitions even on first mount. Would let us use max-height or opacity transitions on lazy-mounted children without always-mounting.

- **Flatten then render**: instead of recursive component tree, flatten the visible rows into an array at the tree root (depth-first, filtered by expanded set), render with a single For loop over flat rows. This gives O(1) component overhead per node, enables windowing, and makes expand-all a pure data operation.

## Acceptance criteria

Chosen approach is validated in a local prototype (not necessarily the full implementation). Document: the approach, the library/pattern used (if any), and any trade-offs around animation vs. expand-all vs. virtualization.
notes:
Files: web/src/pages/SBOMDetail/PackagesTab.tsx
Approach: Ported the flat-DFS pattern already proven in DiffTreeView.tsx to DependencyTreeView. Deleted TreeNodeRow entirely. Centralized expansion state as createSignal(new Set<string>(), {equals:false}). Single visibleRows createMemo does DFS with a pathSet for cycle detection (replaces the nextVisited prop cascade). For loop renders flat rows — O(1) reactive primitives per node, no cascade. Added Expand All / Collapse All buttons; expand-all is one batch Set fill with no reactive cascade.
close_reason: Ported flat-DFS pattern from DiffTreeView to DependencyTreeView; TreeNodeRow deleted; expand-all is a single Set fill with no reactive cascade

title: 1.1 — Production K8s deployment
description:
MVP scope: get a deployable, publicly-reachable version of OCIDex running on the Pi Talos cluster behind the existing Cloudflare Gateway at https://ocidex.app. Hardening (NetworkPolicy, ExternalSecrets, Loki shipping, Prometheus alerts, HA Postgres) is intentionally deferred — those return as later 1.1 / 1.x work once the app is live.

Workstreams (see children):
  A. Image production — multi-arch (linux/amd64,linux/arm64) builds to GHCR via GitHub Actions; in-cluster zot via Tekton as follow-up.
  B. k8s/base manifest gaps — web (nginx) Deployment+Service, prod Postgres (StatefulSet+PVC), goose-migrate Job, k8s/overlays/prod, ServiceMonitor.
  C. Configuration & secrets — DEPLOYMENT.md env-var contract, GitHub OAuth app for ocidex.app, SOPS-encrypted secret values.
  D. Flux app in homelab — homelab/talos-cluster/flux/apps/ocidex/ wired into apps.kustomization.yaml; HTTPRoute referencing cloudflare-gateway.
  E. First deploy + smoke test (capstone).

The only retained 1.0 → 1.1 hard dependency is ocidex-ujj.24 (secrets template); the rest of 1.0 is mostly docs and does not block deploy.

Domain note: originally scoped against ocidex.pfenerty.com; switched to ocidex.app (same Cloudflare account, same cloudflare-gateway/tunnel, same edge-TLS pattern — no extra infra work).
notes: All P1/P2 deliverables complete: multi-arch CI, k8s manifests, Flux deployment, SOPS secrets, DNS, smoke test, release pipeline. PodMonitor (ocidex-0my.7) deferred to a future monitoring epic.
close_reason: All P1/P2 items complete; monitoring deferred to later epic

title: 1.1.A.1 — GitHub Actions multi-arch image builds to GHCR
description:
Add .github/workflows/images.yml that, on push to main, uses docker/setup-qemu-action + docker/setup-buildx-action + docker/build-push-action to build linux/amd64 + linux/arm64 images and push to GHCR. Four images, all from existing Dockerfiles:

  - ghcr.io/pfenerty/ocidex-api               (docker/Dockerfile target=api)
  - ghcr.io/pfenerty/ocidex-scanner-worker    (docker/Dockerfile target=scanner-worker)
  - ghcr.io/pfenerty/ocidex-enrichment-worker (docker/Dockerfile target=enrichment-worker)
  - ghcr.io/pfenerty/ocidex-web               (docker/web/Dockerfile)

Tags: 'sha-<short>' AND 'main'. Auth via GITHUB_TOKEN + permissions: packages: write. No release tagging yet — that comes later.

This is the unblock for the Pi cluster pulling real images. Talos is arm64, so arm64 must be in the matrix from day one.
notes:
Files: .github/workflows/images.yml
Approach: matrix over 4 targets (api, scanner-worker, enrichment-worker, web) on ubuntu-latest. docker/setup-qemu-action@v3 + docker/setup-buildx-action@v3 + docker/build-push-action@v6, platforms linux/amd64,linux/arm64, tags via docker/metadata-action@v5 ('main' branch tag + 'sha-<short>'). GHA cache scoped per image. Concurrency group images-<ref> with cancel-in-progress=false so a half-pushed manifest list does not happen. Login to ghcr.io via GITHUB_TOKEN, permissions.packages: write at workflow level.
Follow-up (human): after first successful run, flip the 4 GHCR packages to Public via the GitHub web UI so Talos can pull without imagePullSecrets. Relevant for issues .11 (Flux app) and .14 (smoke deploy).

title: 1.1.D.1 — Flux app: homelab/talos-cluster/flux/apps/ocidex/
description:
Create the new Flux app directory in the homelab repo, modeled on flux/apps/podinfo/ and flux/apps/zot/:

Files:
  - namespace.yaml: Namespace 'ocidex'
  - kustomization.yaml: aggregates the files below
  - flux-kustomization.yaml: kustomize.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v1 Kustomization that points at GitRepository ref of pfenerty/ocidex repo, path=k8s/overlays/prod, targetNamespace=ocidex, prune=true, interval=10m. Add the GitRepository source separately (or reuse if one exists for ocidex CI).
  - httproute.yaml: HTTPRoute 'ocidex' in namespace ocidex, parentRef cloudflare-gateway/cloudflare-gateway, hostnames=[ocidex.app], rules: prefix '/api' → ocidex-api:80, default '/' → ocidex-web:80

Wire into flux/apps.kustomization.yaml as a new entry. Reference patterns: flux/apps/podinfo/route.yaml (hostname/parentRef shape) and flux/apps/zot/helmrelease.yaml.values.httproute (multi-rule structure).
notes:
Files (in pfenerty/homelab, commits 5d4e460 + fix 6c9cb48):
- talos-cluster/flux/apps/ocidex/namespace.yaml — Namespace 'ocidex'
- talos-cluster/flux/apps/ocidex/git.source.yaml — GitRepository 'ocidex-prod' in flux-system, https://github.com/pfenerty/ocidex.git @ main, 5m. Named '-prod' to avoid collision with the existing 'ocidex' GitRepository in ocidex-ci ns (tracks 'tektonic' branch for Tekton).
- talos-cluster/flux/apps/ocidex/httproute.yaml — HTTPRoute attached to cloudflare-gateway/cloudflare-gateway, hostname ocidex.app, /api → ocidex-api:80, / → ocidex-web:80.
- talos-cluster/flux/apps/ocidex/flux-kustomization.yaml — INNER Flux Kustomization 'ocidex-app' reconciling ./k8s/overlays/prod from sourceRef ocidex-prod into the ocidex namespace.
- talos-cluster/flux/apps/ocidex/kustomization.yaml — local aggregator: namespace + git.source + flux-kustomization + httproute. (.12 will add secrets.yaml.)
- talos-cluster/flux/ocidex.kustomization.yaml — OUTER Flux Kustomization 'ocidex' reconciling ./talos-cluster/flux/apps/ocidex from sourceRef flux-system; dependsOn cilium + cloudflare-gateway; SOPS decryption wired for .12's secret.
- talos-cluster/flux/kustomization.yaml — registers ocidex.kustomization.yaml.

Approach: two-layer reconciliation matching the podinfo pattern. Root flux-system Kustomization → applies the outer ocidex Flux Kustomization → which applies apps/ocidex/ contents (namespace, ocidex-prod GitRepo, HTTPRoute, inner Flux Kustomization) → which applies ocidex repo's k8s/overlays/prod into the ocidex namespace.

Gotcha (fix 6c9cb48): initial commit (5d4e460) had a chicken-and-egg bug — the root Flux Kustomization pointed directly at the ocidex repo's k8s/overlays/prod with sourceRef=ocidex-prod, but the ocidex-prod GitRepository was defined under apps/ocidex/ which nothing reconciled. Sources for an external repo must be applied by an outer Kustomization sourcing the homelab repo itself before any inner Kustomization can use them — same pattern as podinfo's path=./talos-cluster/flux/apps/podinfo with sourceRef=flux-system.

title: 1.1.D.3 — Cloudflare DNS for ocidex.app
description:
Verify how the cluster's Cloudflare Gateway resolves new hostnames. ocidex.app is in the same Cloudflare account as pfenerty.com so the existing tunnel + gateway apply unchanged. Two possibilities:
  (a) The cloudflared tunnel + Cloudflare Gateway API controller auto-manages DNS for any hostname listed in an HTTPRoute attached to cloudflare-gateway (zero-touch).
  (b) The DNS CNAME / tunnel hostname must be added manually in the Cloudflare dashboard.

Check by inspecting flux/apps/podinfo (does podinfo.pfenerty.com 'just work' or was it manually configured?) and the cloudflare-gateway/install.yaml controller config. If (b), add the CNAME for the apex ocidex.app pointing at the tunnel via Cloudflare dashboard (and verify proxy/orange-cloud setting matches what podinfo uses).
close_reason: Cloudflare gateway controller (pl4nty/cloudflare-kubernetes-gateway) auto-manages DNS from HTTPRoute.hostnames. Verified by absence of any manual DNS records for podinfo.pfenerty.com / oci.pfenerty.com in the homelab repo. No CNAME work required for ocidex.app — listing the hostname in the HTTPRoute (added in ocidex-0my.11) is sufficient.

description:

title: Ship k8s/overlays/prod as Flux OCI artifact from CI
description:
## Why

Using a mutable :main image tag with imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent causes nodes to cache stale images, making deploys non-deterministic and rollbacks ambiguous. The fix is to publish the prod kustomization as an OCI artifact from CI with pinned commit-SHA image tags baked in, and have the homelab OCIRepository watch it instead of a GitRepository.

## What

### In ocidex CI (GitHub Actions):
1. After building images, tag them with the commit SHA (ghcr.io/pfenerty/ocidex-{api,scanner-worker,enrichment-worker,web}:$SHA) in addition to :main
2. Run 'kustomize edit set image' on k8s/overlays/prod to replace :main tags with :$SHA
3. Push k8s/overlays/prod as an OCI artifact: oci://ghcr.io/pfenerty/ocidex-manifests:$SHA, also tag :main
4. Use 'flux push artifact' + 'flux tag artifact'

### In homelab repo:
1. Replace the ocidex GitRepository (source for ocidex-app Kustomization) with an OCIRepository pointing to oci://ghcr.io/pfenerty/ocidex-manifests:main
2. Add a GHCR image pull secret if needed (or use provider: github)
3. Update the ocidex-app Kustomization to reference the OCIRepository source

## Acceptance
- git push to ocidex main triggers CI that pushes both app images and an OCI manifest artifact, all tagged with the commit SHA
- Homelab Flux picks up the new artifact within 1 minute and rolls the deployment
- kubectl get pods shows the new image SHA on running pods
- Rollback = pushing a previous artifact SHA tag; no git revert needed

title: Build per-project Flox dev image via GitHub Actions (flox containerize)
description:

Done so far:

New: standalone reusable project ~/code/devbox/ = Debian base + Flox + git + direnv + sshd + baked non-root patrick user (real /etc, containerd-native), plus a Helm chart (chart/) for workspace + Tailscale sidecar (serve TCP 22->127.0.0.1:2222, no node ports) and a GHA arm64 build. ocidex dev-image.yml + the openssh/direnv added to ocidex .flox are superseded. Next: build ghcr.io/pfenerty/devbox then deploy via chart / Flux HelmRelease.
close_reason: Done — reusable devbox image+chart deployed. Standalone ~/code/devbox project (Debian base + Flox + git + direnv + sshd, non-root 'dev' user), published as ghcr.io/pfenerty/devbox (image) + ghcr.io/pfenerty/charts/devbox (OCI Helm chart). Deployed in homelab dev-spaces via Flux OCIRepository+HelmRelease (chart 0.2.0), Tailscale sidecar (serve TCP 22->127.0.0.1:2222, no node ports). Three root causes solved: (1) flox-containerize Nix fake-nss symlinks rejected by containerd -> Debian base; (2) chown EPERM from NFS root_squash default SC -> local-path; (3) pubkey denied for key-only account under UsePAM no -> UsePAM yes. SSH dev@devbox.<tailnet>.ts.net works.

title: fix(web): Caddyfile try_files ordering breaks /auth/* proxy
close_reason: Restructured Caddyfile from flat reverse_proxy+try_files directives to handle blocks. In Caddy's canonical directive order, try_files (pos 14) runs before reverse_proxy (pos 28), so /auth/* and /api/* requests were rewritten to /index.html before the proxy rules could match, causing the SPA to load at /auth/login and show 404.

title: 1.1.B.3 — goose-migrate Job in k8s/base
description:
Add k8s/base/migrate-job.yaml: a Kubernetes Job that runs 'goose -dir db/migrations postgres "$DATABASE_URL" up' against the in-cluster Postgres before the API starts serving.

Approach options (decide during implementation):
  (a) Use the API image with command override — but the API image is distroless/static and doesn't have goose. Either bake goose into a dedicated migrate image (extra Docker target), OR
  (b) Add a 'migrate' target to docker/Dockerfile that copies the goose binary + db/migrations/ into a distroless image (cleanest). Run goose binary directly.

Option (b) recommended. Job's restartPolicy: OnFailure, backoffLimit: 3. envFrom: secretRef ocidex-secrets so DATABASE_URL is available.

Idempotency: Flux re-applies on each reconcile but a completed Job is immutable. Use a generated name (kustomize secretGenerator-style hash) or include a timestamp annotation that forces recreation when migrations change. Simplest: bump a label / use generateName during overlay rendering when migrations change. Investigate flux's 'force' annotation behavior here.
notes:
Files: docker/Dockerfile (new migrate stage + goose install in builder), k8s/base/migrate-job.yaml (new), k8s/base/kustomization.yaml, .github/workflows/images.yml (matrix entry), Tiltfile (build + k8s_resource).
Approach: New 'migrate' Dockerfile target — distroless static image with the goose v3.27.0 CLI binary and db/migrations/ baked in. ENTRYPOINT is the goose CLI prefix; Job supplies DATABASE_URL + 'up' via args (k8s $(VAR) expansion from envFrom: ocidex-secrets). Flux force annotation handles Job immutability across re-applies. backoffLimit=5 with restartPolicy=OnFailure covers Postgres-not-ready on first deploy. Startup migrations in cmd/ocidex/main.go kept as idempotent safety net (per planning decision). Verified with kubectl kustomize on the dev overlay; docker build deferred to CI (no docker available locally).

title: dev-up/dev-down don't handle stale tilt processes
description: make dev-up fails with 'address already in use' when a tilt process is already running — either a suspended foreground job from Ctrl-Z or an orphaned daemon from a prior session. make dev-down only runs 'tilt down' (which removes cluster resources) and never terminates the long-running tilt process, so it accumulates. Both targets should detect and clean up stale tilt processes.
notes:
Files: Makefile (dev-up, dev-down targets)
Approach: Added a preflight to dev-up that detects existing tilt processes (pgrep -x tilt) and stops them with SIGTERM, falling back to SIGKILL after 5s. dev-down now runs tilt down first to clean up cluster resources, then kills the long-running tilt process the same way (previously dev-down only ran tilt down, leaving the daemon orphaned). Handles both stale-from-prior-session daemons and suspended foreground jobs (Ctrl-Z).

title: Migration 00026 hangs on production-size component table
description: The first version of migration 00026 (UNKNOWN main-module backfill) ran regexp_replace per row across the full component table. With 874k rows it never finished — production startup hung 20+ min between 'database connected' and the HTTP server starting, so /auth/login returned 502 Bad Gateway from nginx. Fix: drive the UPDATE from the small main_module CTE inside a DO loop, use the existing idx_component_sbom_id index, and replace regex match with LIKE prefixes. Also restrict to type IN ('library','application') so the 785k file rows (legitimately NULL version) are skipped. Manually verified on the live DB: full migration runs in ~5s instead of >20m.
acceptance_criteria: Migration runs in seconds on a 1M-row component table. ocidex-app starts cleanly, /auth/login returns 307 to GitHub OAuth instead of 502.
notes: Files: db/migrations/00026_backfill_unknown_main_module_version.sql (rewritten as DO loop), tests/main_module_backfill_test.go (replay updated to match). Root cause: original migration ran regexp_replace on every row of component (874k rows in prod), which the planner couldn't push down past the WHERE clause. Fix drives the UPDATE from the small main_module CTE inside a DO loop (one UPDATE per SBOM with a source label), uses idx_component_sbom_id, and matches by LIKE prefix instead of regex. Filters version IN ('UNKNOWN','') and type IN ('library','application') to skip the 785k file rows whose NULL version is legitimate. Verified on the live DB: full migration runs in ~5s instead of >20m. ocidex-app starts cleanly post-migration; /auth/login returns 307 to GitHub OAuth as expected.

title: Clean up BuildInsecureResolver / BuildCredentialResolver in service/registry.go
description:
The two 'Build*Resolver' functions in internal/service/registry.go (lines 107-155) have both naming and implementation issues.

## Naming problems

- 'InsecureResolver' reads like 'a resolver that is insecure' or 'security-aware resolver'. It actually answers: 'is this host configured to be reached over plain HTTP / with TLS verification disabled?'
- Both functions return bare `func(...) ...` closures rather than named types, so call sites lose any semantic hint.

Proposed renames (open to discussion):
- `BuildInsecureResolver` → `BuildInsecureHostLookup` returning a named type `HostInsecurityChecker func(host string) bool`
- `BuildCredentialResolver` → `BuildCredentialLookup` returning a named type `HostCredentialLookup func(host string) (username, token string)`

## Implementation problems

1. **Re-queries every registry on every call.** Each invocation calls `svc.List(ctx, {IsAdmin: true})`, which hits the DB. These resolvers fire on every OCI pull — needs a cache (with invalidation when registries change, likely via the event bus) or at least a short-TTL memoization.
2. **Inline URL parsing duplicated.** Both functions reinvent the same 'strip scheme + trim trailing slash' logic. Extract a helper like `registryHost(r Registry) string`.
3. **`context.Background()` hardcoded inside the closure.** Discards caller context — no cancellation, no deadline, no tracing. The closure signature should accept a `context.Context`.

## Acceptance

- Resolver names make their purpose obvious without reading the body
- Named function types instead of bare `func(...) ...`
- Shared URL-normalization helper (single source of truth)
- Closures accept `context.Context`
- Some form of caching so OCI hot paths don't hit the DB per call
- Existing tests in internal/service/registry_test.go updated; `make check` passes
notes: Files: internal/service/registry.go, internal/enrichment/oci/oci.go, internal/enrichment/ocivalidate/validator.go, cmd/ocidex/main.go, cmd/enrichment-worker/main.go\nApproach: Renamed BuildInsecureResolver→BuildInsecureHostLookup and BuildCredentialResolver→BuildCredentialLookup, returning named types HostInsecurityChecker and HostCredentialLookup. Extracted registryHost() helper to eliminate duplicated URL-stripping. Added closure-held 30s TTL cache (sync.Mutex + map + time.Time) to both builders — eliminates per-OCI-pull DB queries. Threaded context.Context through all resolver call sites (was context.Background()). Updated ocivalidate/oci enricher option func signatures and insecureFor helpers. All tests updated and 2 cache-hit tests added.

title: Jobs tab: default filter to running/queued, add state and registry filter controls
description:
Admin > Jobs currently shows ALL jobs with no filtering UI, paginated but unfiltered. In normal operation the most interesting view is active jobs (running + queued). Completed and failed jobs are only relevant for debugging. Also, the ScanJobResponse has registry_id and repository fields that aren't used for filtering today.

**What needs to change:**

1. Default state filter: initialise the state filter signal to show running+queued jobs (pass state='running' by default to useListScanJobs). Note: the API only accepts a single state value — to show both running and queued, either fetch both in parallel and merge client-side, or default to 'running' only and let users select 'queued' separately. Prefer the two-request approach for accuracy; merge and sort by created_at desc.

2. Filter bar (above the table): add controls for:
   - State: segmented toggle or a <select> with options: All | Queued | Running | Succeeded | Failed (default: Running)
   - Search/filter by repository string (text input, client-side filter on job.repository and job.tag)
   - Registry: a <select> populated from useListRegistries(), filtering client-side by registry_id (since the API does not support this param today). Show 'All registries' as default.

3. Show job counts per state somewhere (can borrow from the scan_jobs stats already shown in the Status tab).

**Affected files:**
- web/src/pages/Admin.tsx — JobsTab function and useListScanJobs call
- web/src/api/queries/jobs.ts — may need to support parallel queries or rethink the accessor API
notes: Files: web/src/pages/admin/JobsTab.tsx\nApproach: Two parallel queries for active mode (running+queued merged client-side, sorted by created_at desc). Client-side repo text filter and registry_id filter. Registry dropdown populated from useListRegistries. Pagination hidden in active mode.

title: Registries: hide webhook-specific actions for poll-only registries
description:
In Admin > Registries, the table always shows 'Copy URL' and 'Regen Secret' buttons for every registry, even for types that do not support webhooks (docker and ghcr have webhook: false in TYPE_CAPS). This is confusing — those registries are poll-only and have no webhook URL or secret to manage.

**What needs to change:**
Conditionally render the webhook action buttons based on whether the registry actually uses webhooks. A registry uses webhooks when scan_mode is 'webhook' or 'both', AND when its type supports it (TYPE_CAPS[type].webhook === true).

The column/cell should:
- Still always render the Webhook URL column (to avoid layout shifts), but show a '-' or empty cell for poll-only registries
- Hide both 'Copy URL' and 'Regen Secret' buttons when the registry's effective scan_mode is 'poll' or the registry type has webhook: false

Condition to use (on each row):
  const hasWebhook = (reg) => reg.scan_mode !== 'poll' && TYPE_CAPS[reg.type as RegType]?.webhook === true

Wrap the two webhook buttons in a Show component using this predicate.

**Affected file:**
- web/src/pages/Admin.tsx — RegistriesTab table row, the 'Webhook URL' cell (lines ~824–843)
notes: Files: web/src/pages/admin/RegistriesTab.tsx Approach: Added hasWebhook(reg) predicate using TYPE_CAPS[type].webhook and scan_mode. Wrapped Copy URL + Regen Secret buttons in —>. The always renders to avoid layout shifts; only the button content is conditional.

title: Diff & tree display: backend-computed, flavor-aware
description:
Overhaul of SBOM diff + dependency-tree UX. Design principles:
1. Tree is the default; flat is the audit view; both stay one click apart.
2. Header summaries must reconcile with what is visible — per-ancestor rollups in tree.
3. Files are not packages — exclude server-side everywhere.
4. Backend does the heavy lifting; UI just renders.
5. Flavor (alpine/debian/wolfi/distroless/...) is a first-class timeline axis derived from SBOM contents at ingest, not from tag parsing.
6. No silent fallbacks — empty states are explicit and offer the flat view.

Phases:
A. Correctness fixes (file noise in changelog, badge palette, unsafe root fallback, empty-tree handling).
B. Backend diff enrichment — direction, descendantChanges, nodeRef, roots, isDirect, expanded ChangeSummary.
C. Frontend simplification — drop debVersionCompare/classifyChange/adjacency build, render directly from B.
D. Tree controls — centralized expansion state, expand-all-changed, show-context toggle, show-transitive toggle, localStorage prefs.
E. Identity tightening — survivor-guard for versioned-name reconcile, purl qualifiers in key.
F. Flavor axis — sbom.flavor column, ingest-time detection (OS props + purl fingerprint + tag fallback), changelog grouping, UI tabs.
G. Direct vs transitive — anchor tree on metadata.component.bom-ref, isDirect flag, direct-default view.
H. Docs + ADRs.

See conversation transcript and ocidex-0ji (tree perf spike) for context.
close_reason: All 38 issues complete. Backend-computed diff tree, flavor axis, identity tightening, tree controls, and docs all shipped.

title: ADR: Diff identity model (purl-base + qualifiers)
description: Document how a component's identity is computed for diff matching. Cover: purl-without-version when present, qualifier inclusion (?distro, ?arch, ?repository_url), fallback (type, group, name) tuple, name-suffix normalization rule (regex -[0-9][0-9.]*$), survivor-guard for the reconcile pass. Output: docs/adr/019-diff-identity-model.md.
notes:
Files: docs/adr/0019-diff-identity-model.md
Approach: Layered identity model with three rules — (1) primary key = purl-base + identity-bearing qualifiers (distro, arch, epoch, repository_url) with alphabetical ordering and an explicit allowlist, noise qualifiers like download_url/checksum dropped; (2) fallback = (type, group, name) tuple when no purl; (3) versioned-name post-pass with a symmetric survivor guard so multi-version installs (gcc-11+gcc-12 → gcc-12+gcc-13) don't false-collapse. ADR documents normative rules, lays out the qualifier policy as a partition table, and references downstream issues bqh.25/26/27 (E1/E2/E3) for implementation + tests. CLAUDE.md ADR-summary table update is deferred to bqh.38 (H2).

title: ADR: Image flavor detection from SBOM contents
description: Document layered flavor detection at ingest time: (1) OS metadata properties (syft:image:os:name etc.), (2) purl-type fingerprint, (3) tag suffix fallback. Document apk-ambiguity resolution via ?distro qualifier, distroless handling, and known limitations (multi-distro images, tools that omit OS metadata). Output: docs/adr/020-image-flavor-axis.md.
notes:
Files: docs/adr/0020-image-flavor-axis.md
Approach: Layered SBOM-content detection at ingest time, persisted on sbom.flavor (text, indexed). Layer 1 = OS metadata properties (syft:image:os:name, version-id; Trivy equivalents; CycloneDX 1.5+ structured fields) — most precise, distinguishes alpine-3.18 vs alpine-3.19. Layer 2 = dominant purl-type fingerprint (apk/deb/rpm) with ?distro qualifier disambiguation for the apk-family ambiguity (alpine vs wolfi vs chainguard). Layer 3 = tag-suffix heuristic from a hardcoded suffix list (last resort). Schema is single lowercased string of form <family> or <family>-<version_id>; sentinel 'unknown' for unresolved (non-NULL so flavor stays a non-nullable grouping axis). Manual override deferred to a future IngestParams.Flavor field. Tag-suffix parsing rejected as primary because conventions vary too much across codebases; manual config rejected for backfill cost.

Persist on sbom.flavor. Out of scope: per-artifact override pattern.
notes:
Files: internal/service/flavor.go (new), internal/service/flavor_test.go (new), internal/service/sbom.go, db/queries/sbom.sql, internal/repository/sbom.sql.go (generated)

Also added UpdateSBOMFlavor query for F3 backfill. 24 unit tests covering all ADR-0020 scenarios.

title: ADR: Backend-computed diff tree and rollups
description: Document the principle that DiffTree responses are pre-computed on the backend: roots, isDirect flags, descendantChanges aggregates, nodeRef cross-references, ComponentDiff.direction. Frontend renders only. Output: docs/adr/021-backend-computed-diff-tree.md.
notes:
Files: docs/adr/0021-backend-computed-diff-tree.md
Approach: Enrich the existing DiffTree response in place (rejected: separate /diff-tree/structured endpoint, status quo). Six normative additions: (1) roots[] computed from metadata.component.bom-ref's outgoing edges, falling back to zero-in-edge nodes, never to 'first 10' guess; (2) isDirect on each ComponentSummary (1-hop from synthetic root); (3) direction on each ComponentDiff (added/removed/upgraded/downgraded/modified) using a deb-aware comparator — replaces frontend classifyChange/debVersionCompare; (4) nodeRef on each ComponentDiff for O(1) join to graph nodes — replaces fragile frontend name/purl/bom-ref maps; (5) descendantChanges aggregate per node from a single DFS at the tree root — drives rollup chips; (6) ChangeSummary expanded to include upgraded+downgraded. Cycle handling normative (visit-once per ancestor). Wire growth ~2% on a typical 500-component SBOM. Backwards-compatible (additive) with B-then-C ordering enforced via beads deps so frontend never depends on a missing field.

title: Architecture switcher shows duplicates for Windows multi-arch SBOMs
description:
## Problem

When clicking into a Windows multi-arch artifact version (/sboms/{id}), the architecture switcher shows duplicate buttons (e.g., "amd64", "amd64", "amd64", "arm64"). Same problem appears in the Architectures column of the artifact detail Versions table.

## Evidence

- Arch switcher (web/src/pages/SBOMDetail/index.tsx:276-290) renders one button per sibling SBOM with the same subjectVersion. No dedup on architecture.
- Scanner ociExpandIndex (internal/scanner/catalog.go:360-380) parses each manifest in an OCI image index but stores only arch. Windows multi-arch indexes contain multiple manifests sharing architecture: amd64 but differing in os.version.
- ociGetImageMetadata (internal/scanner/catalog.go:391-470) reads only config.Architecture — same problem.
- Result: multiple SBOMs per Windows variant; identical architecture enrichment; UI renders each as a separate "amd64" button.

## Root cause

Scanner and enrichment discard os and os.version. No qualifier capable of distinguishing Windows variants.

## Fix direction (two layers)

1. Scanner/enrichment: capture os and os.version alongside architecture. Option (a): extend enrichment record with explicit os/os.version fields, update SBOM list query. Option (b): composite platform string (e.g., windows/amd64/10.0.17763) when os == windows.
2. Frontend defense: dedupe arch siblings by platform identifier in SBOMDetail/index.tsx:276-290 and SBOMsTab.tsx:178-194.

## Affected files

- internal/scanner/catalog.go
- internal/enrichment/ (oci-metadata enricher)
- db/queries/artifact.sql (if option 1a)
- web/src/pages/SBOMDetail/index.tsx
- web/src/pages/ArtifactDetail/SBOMsTab.tsx
notes: Files: internal/enrichment/oci/oci.go, web/src/pages/SBOMDetail/index.tsx\nApproach: Two-layer fix. (1) Enricher: added platformArchitecture() helper that builds composite 'windows/amd64/10.0.17763' string when OS=windows and os.version is present, using cfg.OSVersion from go-containerregistry v1.ConfigFile. Stored in enrichment JSONB architecture field; SQL queries unchanged. (2) Frontend: archSiblings() now deduplicates by architecture using a Set — handles existing SBOMs re-enriched before this fix and any edge cases.

title: Disambiguate overloaded 'Registry' naming across packages
description:
The word 'registry' is used for three unrelated concepts in the codebase, which makes main.go and surrounding wiring harder to read for newcomers:

1. `service.RegistryService` / `registrySvc` — CRUD over container/OCI registry configurations (zot, harbor, ghcr). User-facing domain object, DB-backed. Lives in internal/service/ and internal/api/types.go.
2. `extension.Registry` / `reg` — in-process plugin lifecycle manager (Init/Start/Stop). Lives in internal/extension/registry.go.
3. `enrichment.Registry` / `enrichReg` — in-process lookup map of enricher implementations by name. Lives in internal/enrichment/registry.go.

All three appear within ~10 lines of cmd/ocidex/main.go (lines 88-94), with only variable naming distinguishing them.

Proposal: rename the two non-domain 'Registry' types so 'Registry' unambiguously refers to the container-registry domain concept. Candidates:
- `extension.Registry` → `extension.Manager` or `extension.Host` (lifecycle coordinator)
- `enrichment.Registry` → `enrichment.Catalog` or `enrichment.Index` (name-keyed lookup)

Scope:
- Rename types and constructors
- Update call sites (main.go and any internal consumers)
- Update tests
- No behavior change

Acceptance:
- 'Registry' in code refers only to OCI/container registries
- `make check` passes
- Newcomer reading main.go can tell what each variable means without grep
notes:
Files: internal/extension/registry.go, internal/enrichment/registry.go, internal/enrichment/dispatcher.go, internal/extension/registry_test.go, internal/enrichment/dispatcher_test.go, cmd/ocidex/main.go, cmd/enrichment-worker/main.go, cmd/scanner-worker/main.go, tests/scan_to_enrich_test.go
Approach: extension.Registry→extension.Manager (NewRegistry→NewManager); enrichment.Registry→enrichment.Catalog (NewRegistry→NewCatalog). Pure rename, no behavior change. make check passes (0 lint issues, all 14 packages ok, 63 frontend tests pass).

title: NATS StatefulSet missing server_name; JetStream cluster cannot start
description:
In a 3-replica JetStream cluster, nats-server requires each pod to have a unique server_name. The base manifest at k8s/base/nats-statefulset.yaml (args: -js, -cluster, -routes) does not set --name, so pods crashloop with:

  nats-server: jetstream cluster requires server_name to be set

Observed on rpi-cluster after first prod deploy (ocidex-0my.11). nats-0 and nats-1 both in CrashLoopBackOff.

Fix: add downward-API POD_NAME env and pass --name $(POD_NAME) to nats-server args. Standard pattern:

  env:
    - name: POD_NAME
      valueFrom:
        fieldRef:
          fieldPath: metadata.name
  args:
    - --name
    - $(POD_NAME)
    - -js
    - -cluster
    - nats://0.0.0.0:6222
    - -routes
    - nats://nats-0.nats:6222,nats://nats-1.nats:6222,nats://nats-2.nats:6222

Also worth checking whether the embedded-mode (single replica) overlays need different args.

Blocks ocidex-0my.14 (smoke deploy).
notes:
Files: k8s/base/nats-statefulset.yaml.
Approach: added downward-API POD_NAME env (fieldRef metadata.name) and prepended '--name $(POD_NAME)' to the nats-server args so each pod in the 3-replica cluster gets a unique server_name; K8s expands $(POD_NAME) in args because it is a defined env var in the same container. Base-only change — prod overlay uses base directly (where the crashloop occurred); dev overlay replaces args with [-js] (single node) and is unaffected (verified via kubectl kustomize: prod shows --name $(POD_NAME); dev still replicas=1, args -js). Cluster confirmation on rpi-cluster (nats-0/1/2 Ready, no 'requires server_name' log) is the deploy-time step.
close_reason: Added --name $(POD_NAME) + downward-API POD_NAME env to k8s/base/nats-statefulset.yaml; kustomize renders verified for prod (clustered) and dev (single node).

title: Jobs tab: error detail collapses on every data refresh
description:
In Admin > Jobs, clicking 'View error' on a failed job opens a <details> element showing the error text. However, useListScanJobs has refetchInterval: 2500, which triggers a full table re-render every 2.5 seconds. Because <details> is an uncontrolled element in Solid, each re-render resets it to its default closed state, collapsing the expanded error immediately.

**Root cause:**
The <details> element in the Last Error column (Admin.tsx:944–950) is uncontrolled HTML. Solid's reconciliation replaces the DOM node on each query result update, losing the open attribute.

**Fix:**
Replace the uncontrolled <details> with a controlled expand/collapse using createSignal per job row. Track a Set or Map of expanded job IDs in the JobsTab component, and use a Show + button pattern instead of <details>/<summary>.

Example approach:
- In JobsTab, add: const [expandedErrors, setExpandedErrors] = createSignal(new Set<string>())
- In the row: replace <details> with a button that toggles the job id in the set, and a Show component that renders the error text when the id is present
- Use untrack or a stable keyed accessor so the signal survives the query update

**Affected file:**
- web/src/pages/Admin.tsx — JobsTab, the 'Last Error' column cell (lines ~943–951)
close_reason: replaced uncontrolled <details> with Set<string> signal in JobsTab scope; expanded state survives query refetches

title: dev-up doesn't stop conflicting docker-compose containers
description: The legacy docker-compose dev flow (make run / embedded mode) binds host ports 5432, 4222, 5000, 8080, 3000 — all of which Tilt also port-forwards from the cluster. Because postgres in docker-compose.yml has a restart policy, it gets auto-started on Docker daemon start and silently blocks Tilt's postgres port-forward with 'address already in use'. dev-up should stop the docker-compose stack as a preflight.
notes:
Files: Makefile (dev-up target)
Approach: Added a preflight to dev-up that detects a running docker-compose stack ([ -n "$(docker compose ps -q)" ]) and runs 'docker compose stop' before starting Tilt. The two flows share host ports (5432, 4222, 5000, 8080, 3000) so they can't coexist; postgres in particular has a restart policy that brings it back on Docker daemon start, silently re-binding 5432.

title: Package view: filter plan/file-type components by default, with show toggle

**What needs to change:**
Apply a consistent plan-file filter across ALL views that display packages:
1. DependencyTreeView: filter out nodes from the dependency graph where the corresponding ComponentSummary has type === 'file' OR a purl type of 'file', before building the tree adjacency list.

3. DiffEntry (compare/build-history): the 'Packages only' checkbox already filters to items with a purl, but plan files (type 'file') can still appear. Add a secondary filter that hides purl type === 'file' items when the toggle is on.

**UI:**
Add a 'Show plan files' toggle (default off) to:
- The filter bar in the package view (PackagesTab / tree view toolbar)
- The filter bar in DiffEntry (alongside the existing 'Packages only' checkbox)

title: docker/api/Dockerfile only ships /ocidex; worker binaries are missing
description:
docker/api/Dockerfile builds cmd/ocidex, cmd/scanner-worker, and cmd/enrichment-worker in the build stage but only copies /ocidex into the distroless runtime stage. The K8s worker Deployments use 'args: ["/scanner-worker"]' / '["/enrichment-worker"]' which the container then passes as an arg to the ENTRYPOINT /usr/local/bin/ocidex. Result: 'workers' silently run the API binary on port 8080 — they appear Ready, never consume from NATS, and look like dead workers in any distributed-mode test.

Fix: either (a) COPY all three binaries into the runtime image and remove the hard-coded ENTRYPOINT in favour of CMD that the manifest's args replaces, or (b) consolidate to a single binary that dispatches on the first positional argument. (a) keeps the deployments correct as written.

Surfaced during ocidex-pww (local Talos + Tilt dev loop) end-to-end bring-up.
close_reason: Fixed via multi-target Dockerfile (docker/Dockerfile) and per-service image builds: ocidex-api, ocidex-scanner-worker, ocidex-enrichment-worker. Each image ships only its own binary; deployments reference the matching image. Verified end-to-end: scan jobs now move queued → running → succeeded.

title: Backfill 'UNKNOWN' Go main-module version from metadata.component
description: Syft emits version='UNKNOWN' for the Go main module when scanning binaries because Go's runtime/debug.BuildInfo says '(devel)' until a tagged build. The dex SBOM has 'github.com/dexidp/dex' with version='UNKNOWN' even though metadata.component.version is 'v2.31.0'. Fix: at ingest time, when a component's purl base matches the metadata.component (same path/group/name) AND the component version is 'UNKNOWN' or empty, fill it from metadata.component.version. Backfill existing rows via SQL migration.
acceptance_criteria: Re-ingest dex 2.31.0 SBOM: github.com/dexidp/dex shows version v2.31.0. Existing rows backfilled. Backend test covers the rule.
notes: Files: internal/service/main_module.go (new), internal/service/main_module_test.go (new), internal/service/sbom.go (insertBOMContent + insertComponents take mainModule + subjectVersion), db/migrations/00026_backfill_unknown_main_module_version.sql (new), tests/main_module_backfill_test.go (new). Approach: extractMainModulePath() reads syft/trivy image.source label and strips scheme+credentials+.git. effectiveComponentVersion() backfills version=UNKNOWN/empty from subjectVersion when component matches main module by purl path or name (exact match — submodules NOT backfilled). Migration mirrors the rule for existing rows via JSONB extraction + regex on raw_bom. Two integration tests pass: forward ingest (TestMainModuleVersionBackfill) and migration replay (TestMainModuleVersionBackfillMigration).

title: ocidex main requires GITHUB_CLIENT_ID/SECRET and SESSION_SECRET even in worker contexts
description:
The shared ocidex binary refuses to start without GITHUB_CLIENT_ID, GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET, and SESSION_SECRET. Today this only matters because the worker Deployments accidentally run the API binary (see sibling bug on the Dockerfile), but once workers run their own binaries they shouldn't need OAuth config either.

Fix: gate the GITHUB_*/SESSION_SECRET requirement on whether the API server is actually being started. The worker entry points and the embedded-mode non-API paths don't authenticate browser sessions.

Surfaced during ocidex-pww (local Talos + Tilt dev loop) — had to inject these env vars into the dev overlay's worker patches as a workaround.
notes: Verified RESOLVED by binary split (701d7a6). Workers cmd/scanner-worker + cmd/enrichment-worker call config.Load() only; validateOAuthConfig is API-only (cmd/ocidex/main.go:61, migrate subcommand returns before run()). config.go does not mark GITHUB_*/SESSION_SECRET as required (only DATABASE_URL). Runtime check: both worker binaries built and run --once with GITHUB_*/SESSION_SECRET unset start cleanly and fail only at 'pinging database'. Added explicit regression case in internal/config/config_test.go ('OAuth/session vars not required (workers, ocidex-mf3)') so a future env:",required" tag fails a named test.

title: docker/Dockerfile migrate stage COPY uses wrong GOPATH; image has never built
description:
The migrate stage at docker/Dockerfile:43 does:

  COPY --from=builder /root/go/bin/goose /goose

But the upstream golang:1.25-bookworm image sets GOPATH=/go (not $HOME/go). 'go install github.com/pressly/goose/v3/cmd/goose@v3.27.0' in the builder stage writes the binary to /go/bin/goose, so the COPY fails with:

  failed to compute cache key: failed to calculate checksum of ref ...:
    "/root/go/bin/goose": not found

The image has never published to GHCR — that's why ghcr.io/pfenerty/ocidex-migrate appears not to exist. (Earlier hypothesis that the package was private was wrong; GHCR returns the same 403 for missing packages as for private ones.)

Fix: change line 43 to

  COPY --from=builder /go/bin/goose /goose

The cache mounts at lines 11 and 14 ('--mount=type=cache,target=/root/go/pkg/mod' and '--mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/go-build') also point at the wrong path (real module cache is /go/pkg/mod, real build cache is /root/.cache/go-build which is right by accident). They're dead weight — caches that never hit. Not breaking, but worth fixing as a follow-up for build time.

Blocks ocidex-0my.14 (smoke deploy).
notes:
Files: docker/Dockerfile (lines 11,14).
Resolution: the original COPY bug (migrate stage copying /root/go/bin/goose) is moot — ocidex-ujj.59 deleted the migrate stage and goose install entirely, so the image now builds. The only live remnant was the two BuildKit module-cache mounts targeting /root/go/pkg/mod; the golang:1.25-bookworm image sets GOPATH=/go, so 'go mod download' writes to /go/pkg/mod and those mounts never hit. Changed both to /go/pkg/mod. Left --mount target=/root/.cache/go-build as-is (correct: GOCACHE defaults to $HOME/.cache/go-build, HOME=/root). Build-time verification (cache populates, faster rebuilds) is on a docker host; docker is unavailable in this env.
close_reason: COPY bug obsolete (migrate stage removed in ujj.59); fixed remaining wrong module-cache mount paths /root/go/pkg/mod -> /go/pkg/mod in docker/Dockerfile.

title: Compare page: show architecture and build time in SBOM selection dropdowns
description:
The SBOM picker dropdowns on /diff currently use sbomLabel() which only shows 'version · date' or 'date · N components'. When an artifact has multiple SBOMs for the same version (e.g. multi-arch builds), the options are nearly indistinguishable. Architecture and build time are already present on SBOMSummary (fields: architecture and buildDate).

**What needs to change:**
Update the option labels in both the From and To SBOM selects in Diff.tsx to include architecture and build time when present.

Suggested label format:
- With version + arch + buildDate: '24.04 · amd64 · built Jan 15, 2025, 3:42 PM'
- With version + arch only: '24.04 · amd64 · ingested Jan 15, 2025'
- With version only (no arch): '24.04 · Jan 15, 2025'
- Fallback (no version): 'Jan 15, 2025, 3:42 PM · 342 components'

Implement a new sbomPickerLabel(sbom: SBOMSummary) helper in utils/format.ts that produces this richer label. Use it only in Diff.tsx option elements (not sbomLabel which is used in other contexts).

**Affected files:**
- web/src/utils/format.ts — add sbomPickerLabel()
- web/src/pages/Diff.tsx — use sbomPickerLabel() for option text
notes:
Files: web/src/utils/format.ts, web/src/pages/Diff.tsx
Approach: Added sbomPickerLabel() helper that includes architecture and buildDate when present. Uses existing formatDateTime() for build timestamps. Both From and To SBOM selects in Diff.tsx now use sbomPickerLabel() instead of sbomLabel(). sbomLabel() unchanged for other contexts.

title: Add per-message timeout to ProcessOne in NATS extensions
description: Add a per-message processing timeout to the ProcessOne method in internal/extension/ NATS handlers. Prevents a single slow message from blocking the consumer indefinitely.\n\nAffected: internal/extension/nats.go or similar NATS extension consumer.
notes: Files: internal/scanner/nats_extension.go, internal/enrichment/nats_extension.go, cmd/scanner-worker/main.go, cmd/enrichment-worker/main.go, tests/nats_integration_test.go, tests/scan_to_enrich_test.go\nApproach: Added msgTimeout time.Duration field to both NATSExtension structs. NewNATSExtension now takes msgTimeout as a parameter. The processing goroutines wrap ProcessOne with context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), e.msgTimeout) so hung goroutines release the semaphore slot before JetStream redelivers. Scanner uses 9m (AckWait 10m - 1m), enrichment uses 4m (AckWait 5m - 1m). Test call sites updated with matching timeouts.

title: Split internal/scanner: move syft engine to internal/scanner/engine
description:
The API binary imports internal/scanner for ScanRequest/Submitter/Walker types, but the same package also contains the syft-using SyftScanner and in-process Dispatcher. This drags syft + stereoscope + cloud SDKs into the API binary (114MB) even in distributed mode where the API never runs a scan.

Move SyftScanner, Dispatcher, and the wrapping Extension into a new subpackage internal/scanner/engine. Keep ScanRequest, Scanner interface, Submitter/Walker/Poller/NATSSubmitter under internal/scanner. Update cmd/ocidex and cmd/scanner-worker callers.

Plan: /home/patrick/.claude/plans/cached-puzzling-fairy.md (section 1)
Expected: API binary drops ~90MB; syft/stereoscope removed from API dep tree.
notes:
Files: internal/scanner/{scanner.go,metadata.go} (slimmed), internal/scanner/engine/{syft.go,dispatcher.go,extension.go,dispatcher_test.go} (new), cmd/ocidex/main.go (drop engine import, add validateScannerConfig), cmd/scanner-worker/main.go (engine import), tests/scan_to_enrich_test.go (engine import), docker-compose.yml/.env.example/docs/CONFIGURATION.md/CLAUDE.md (doc updates).

Approach: moved Syft-using files (SyftScanner, Dispatcher, Extension) from internal/scanner to new internal/scanner/engine subpackage. Kept ScanRequest + Scanner interface in scanner. Added exported scanner.FillMetadata so engine doesn't need scanner's unexported HTTP helpers. Removed embedded-mode scanner wiring from cmd/ocidex entirely (cmd/ocidex no longer imports engine). Added startup validation: SCANNER_ENABLED=true now requires OCIDEX_MODE=distributed.

Result: API binary 114MB → 16MB (-86%) with -ldflags -s -w. External deps 1647 → 342. syft + stereoscope (152 packages) no longer in API.

title: fix: filter unsupported manifest types (v1+prettyjws) before scan queue

title: fix: preserve original started_at on scan job retry, track last_attempt_at
description: StartScanJob was overwriting started_at on every redelivery, losing the original start time and resetting the timeout clock. Add last_attempt_at column, use COALESCE to preserve started_at, and fix TimeoutScanJobs to use last_attempt_at.
notes:
Files: db/migrations/00028_scan_job_last_attempt.sql, db/queries/scan_jobs.sql, internal/service/job.go, internal/api/types.go, internal/api/jobs.go, web/openapi.json, web/src/types/openapi.d.ts
Approach: Added last_attempt_at column. StartScanJob now uses COALESCE(started_at, now()) so the original start time is preserved across retries; last_attempt_at tracks when the current attempt began. TimeoutScanJobs uses COALESCE(last_attempt_at, started_at) so the timeout window resets per attempt, not per original enqueue. Exposed last_attempt_at in API response.

notes:

Approach: Added SCANNER_MAX_ACK_PENDING and ENRICHMENT_MAX_ACK_PENDING config fields defaulting to maxConc*4. Removed hardcoded enrichmentMaxConcurrency constant; NATSExtension now holds maxConc (semaphore) and maxAckPending (NATS consumer) as separate fields. Updated all test call sites.

title: Outbox refactor: Postgres-as-queue, NATS-as-doorbell

Diagnosed root cause of the prod 1093-queued / 574-failed pile-up: reconciler creating duplicate stream messages because slow-but-not-orphaned jobs cross the 10-min threshold under Pi4 throughput limits.

The dual-write antipattern, orphan reconciler, NATS-side retry/DLQ, MaxAckPending coordination, and Nats-Msg-Id dedup window are all gone. title: EMERGENCY: stop the orphan reconciler in prod
description: The reconciler is currently republishing 100 jobs every 5 min on each scanner-worker pod, creating duplicate stream messages that workers waste cycles re-processing (confirmed by 'skipping duplicate sbom ingestion' log lines). Remove the runJobReconciler goroutine from cmd/scanner-worker/main.go. Ship this independently before the bigger refactor so prod gets relief immediately. The catalog walker + workers will drain the 1093-job backlog naturally over ~45h.
notes: Files: cmd/scanner-worker/main.go\nApproach: Removed the runJobReconciler goroutine startup; left the function body in place for the outbox refactor to delete cleanly. Confirmed via prod logs that the reconciler was claiming exactly 100 jobs per 5-min tick (batchSize cap) and workers were processing 'skipping duplicate sbom ingestion' rows — direct evidence of self-inflicted duplicate work.

title: Outbox queries: id-based Claim/Finish/Fail + stuck-running sweep
description: Add SQL queries that operate on scan_jobs.id (UUID) instead of nats_msg_id: ClaimScanJobByID (UPDATE WHERE id AND state='queued' RETURNING joined registry fields), FinishScanJobByID, FailOrRequeueScanJobByID (increments attempts; sets 'failed' if attempts>=max else 'queued'), RequeueStuckRunning (sweep that moves stale 'running' rows back to 'queued', failing if max retries hit). Add idx_scan_jobs_queued ON (state, created_at) WHERE state='queued' for poll-loop efficiency. Service-layer methods that wrap them. Doesn't change behavior yet — sets up the primitives for the atomic switch in the next issue.
notes: Files: db/migrations/00031_scan_jobs_outbox_indexes.sql, db/queries/scan_jobs.sql, internal/repository/repository.go, internal/service/job.go, internal/scanner/engine/dispatcher_test.go\nApproach: ClaimScanJobByID + ClaimNextQueuedJob both UPDATE...RETURNING with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED. Registry credentials joined into the RETURNING projection so workers need no second query. FailOrRequeueScanJobByID gates retry policy in one place (CASE on attempts vs max). RequeueStuckRunning replaces the orphan reconciler — only stuck-job sweep the outbox model needs. Partial indexes on (state='queued', created_at) and (state='running', last_attempt_at) keep both fast.

title: Atomic switch: workers + producers to outbox pattern
description: Single atomic deploy. (1) Producer (NATSSubmitter, catalog walker): publish payload becomes {"id":"<uuid>"} on subject ocidex.scan.hint. DB row still inserted in same flow but is now the only source of truth. (2) Worker: replace internal/scanner/nats_extension.go's handleMsg with a hint handler that calls processJob(ctx, id). processJob: ClaimScanJobByID → if no row, ack and skip; else Syft scan; on success FinishScanJobByID; on failure FailOrRequeueScanJobByID. (3) Add DB poll loop in scanner-worker running every 30s, calling processJob for each queued row up to maxConc. (4) Consumer config: AckPolicy=AckExplicit (ack on receive), MaxDeliver=1, MaxAckPending small (just buffering). NATS no longer does retry. (5) Replace TimeoutJobs with RequeueStuckRunning in the existing reaper goroutine. (6) Delete: runJobReconciler, NATSSubmitter.Republish, routeToDLQ subject routing, scannerMaxDeliver constant, SCANNER_MAX_ACK_PENDING + ENRICHMENT_MAX_ACK_PENDING config, reconcile_attempts column (down migration). Also handles enrichment-worker analogously.
notes: Files: ~18 files. New: internal/scanner/nats_hint.go, db/migrations/00032_drop_reconcile_attempts.sql. Deleted: internal/scanner/nats_extension.go, internal/scanner/engine/extension.go, tests/nats_scanner_dedup_test.go. Refactored: Dispatcher (pure scan+ingest, returns sbomID), NATSSubmitter (publishes {id} hint only), scanner-worker main (NATSHintExtension + RequeueStuckRunning sweep, no reconciler). Net -702 lines. Verified end-to-end via TestScanToEnrichFlow with the new path.

title: Admin UI: Dead Letter view reads scan_jobs WHERE state='failed'
description: With the outbox refactor, 'failed' is the only DLQ — scan_jobs.state='failed' + last_error. Update the Dead Letter sub-tab in web/src/pages/admin/JobsTab.tsx to query the existing list endpoint with state=failed instead of the /admin/dlq endpoint. Remove the dlq-specific API endpoint (GET /api/v1/admin/dlq) and its query/service plumbing. Add a 'retry' admin action that resets state to 'queued' for a selected row.
notes: Files: db/queries/scan_jobs.sql (RetryScanJob), internal/repository/repository.go, internal/api/{jobs,router,types}.go, internal/service/job.go, web/src/api/queries/{jobs.ts,index.ts}, web/src/pages/admin/JobsTab.tsx\nApproach: Removed GET /api/v1/admin/dlq (DLQ-via-NATS visibility is dead). Removed Dead Letter view toggle in frontend (state filter dropdown already covers 'failed' rows). Added POST /api/v1/admin/jobs/{id}/retry that calls RetryScanJob — resets 'failed' row to 'queued' with cleared attempts/error/timing. Frontend mutation invalidates jobs cache on success. scan_job_failures table kept until ocidex-ujj.74.6 drops it.

title: Docs: rewrite INGESTION Path E + OPERATIONS playbook for outbox
description: Rewrite docs/INGESTION.md Path E to reflect the outbox model: producer inserts scan_jobs row + publishes {id} hint; worker claims via UPDATE...RETURNING; DB poll as fallback. Update docs/OPERATIONS.md: NATS pod down = falls back to polling, no incident; PVC loss = still no incident; stuck-running sweep replaces orphan reconciler. Remove all references to MaxAckPending tuning, Nats-Msg-Id dedup, DLQ-via-NATS subject, reconcile_attempts. Add an ADR (docs/adr/) recording the dual-write→outbox decision.
notes: Files: docs/adr/0024-outbox-pattern-for-scan-queue.md (new), docs/INGESTION.md (Path E rewritten), docs/OPERATIONS.md (rewritten).\nApproach: ADR records context + decision + consequences + alternatives. INGESTION explains producer/consumer flow + knobs + failure-mode matrix. OPERATIONS treats NATS-down as non-incident, documents stuck-running sweep semantics and Retry button workflow.

title: Bulk retry: 'Retry all failed' admin action
description: Per-row retry already exists (ocidex-ujj.74.4). Add a bulk variant for incidents where many rows share a single root cause (e.g. reconciler false-positives in the dead-write era). POST /api/v1/admin/jobs/retry-failed resets all state='failed' rows back to 'queued' with cleared attempts/error/timing; returns affected count. UI shows a 'Retry all failed (N)' button on the JobsTab when state filter is 'Failed'.
notes: Files: db/queries/scan_jobs.sql, internal/repository/repository.go, internal/service/job.go, internal/api/{jobs,types,router}.go, web/src/api/queries/{jobs.ts,index.ts}, web/src/pages/admin/JobsTab.tsx\nApproach: RetryAllFailedScanJobs SQL (UPDATE returning rowcount). Service.RetryAllFailed wraps it. POST /api/v1/admin/jobs/retry-failed admin-gated, returns count. Frontend: button visible only when state filter='failed', confirm dialog + count alert on success.

title: Prod: 3x scanner throughput — postgres CPU, +1 replica, concurrency=2
description: Cluster has ~70% headroom (nodes at 31% CPU, 63% mem). Real bottleneck is postgres CPU pegged at 199m of 200m limit. Bump postgres limits + add 3rd scanner replica + raise SCANNER_MAX_CONCURRENCY to 2. Total scanner concurrency goes from 2 to 6 (3 pods × 2). Memory limit on scanner-worker bumped to 1.5Gi to fit two concurrent Syft processes. CPU limit on scanner-worker bumped to 1500m to avoid throttling.
notes: Files: k8s/overlays/prod/patches/{resources,replicas,env-prod-scanner}.yaml. Verified via kubectl kustomize. Postgres limits 200m/512Mi → 1000m/1Gi; scanner limits 500m/1Gi → 1500m/1.5Gi; scanner replicas 2→3; SCANNER_MAX_CONCURRENCY 1→2. Net effect: 3 pods × 2 concurrent scans = 6 in-flight (was 2).

title: Prod: revert SCANNER_MAX_CONCURRENCY to 1 — Pi4 CPU was the real bottleneck
description: Sequel to ocidex-ujj.76. With concurrency=2 + 3 replicas, node talos-1q6-aue hit 92% CPU and scanner pods OOMKilled / context-deadline-exceeded on ingest. Postgres turned out to be at 137m of 1000m limit — not the bottleneck. The real cap is Pi4 cores: two concurrent Syft processes saturate the node. Revert SCANNER_MAX_CONCURRENCY 2→1; keep 3 replicas (one scan per Pi), postgres limit bump (harmless headroom), and scanner-worker resource bumps (overprovisioned for concurrency=1 but no cost).
notes: Files: k8s/overlays/prod/patches/env-prod-scanner.yaml. Reverted SCANNER_MAX_CONCURRENCY 2→1. Kept replicas=3 and postgres/scanner-worker resource bumps. Net concurrency vs original baseline: 2→3 (+50%) cluster-wide. Pi4 cores remain the actual cap until storage / Syft tuning / hardware addresses it.

title: Migrate Postgres from StatefulSet to CloudNativePG (CNPG)
description: Replace the hand-tuned postgres:15.4-alpine StatefulSet with a CNPG-managed Cluster. Operator provides auto-tuning based on declared resources, continuous WAL backup, PITR, optional pgbouncer pooling, baked-in Prometheus metrics, and managed major-version upgrades. Implementation lives in the homelab flux deployments (talos-cluster/flux). Cutover uses CNPG's initdb.import.monolith to do an in-place pg_dump/restore from the existing StatefulSet, so no manual export step. After cutover verification, the old StatefulSet is removed from the ocidex repo's k8s/base.
notes: All 4 children complete. CNPG migration done end-to-end: operator installed (homelab cnpg.kustomization.yaml), Cluster CR created (homelab apps/ocidex/postgres.yaml), data imported via initdb.import.monolith, DATABASE_URL flipped, apps verified against new DB, legacy StatefulSet removed from ocidex repo.

Add talos-cluster/flux/cnpg.kustomization.yaml. Register in talos-cluster/flux/kustomization.yaml. Renovate will manage the chart version going forward.

Approach: HelmRelease against oci://ghcr.io/cloudnative-pg/charts pinned at 0.24.1 in cnpg-system. Registered in top-level Flux kustomization. ocidex Kustomization now dependsOn cnpg so the operator/CRDs land before the Cluster CR.

title: internal/jobqueue: generic outbox-pattern worker
description: Introduce internal/jobqueue package with a generic Worker[C Claim] that implements the outbox pattern (NATS hint + DB poll fallback + stuck-running sweep). Apply it to scanner jobs (replacing NATSHintExtension) and enrichment jobs (adding enrichment_jobs table to replace the fragile relay-only path). Goal: all job types follow the same durable, DB-backed pattern; Syft stays isolated to cmd/scanner-worker.
notes:
Files: internal/jobqueue/worker.go (new), internal/service/enrichjob.go (new), internal/enrichment/submitter.go (new), db/migrations/00034_enrichment_jobs.sql (new), db/queries/enrichment_jobs.sql (new), internal/repository/enrichment_jobs.sql.go (generated), internal/scanner/nats_hint.go (deleted), internal/enrichment/nats_extension.go (deleted)
Approach: Introduced jobqueue.Worker[C Claim] generic struct implementing the outbox pattern (NATS hint + DB poll fallback + stuck-running sweep). Queue[C Claim] interface carries ClaimByID, ClaimNext, FailOrRequeue, RequeueStuckRunning. Processor is func(ctx, C) error — Finish is called inside the processor so type-specific results (e.g. sbom_id on scan jobs) stay out of the generic layer. enrichment_jobs table mirrors scan_jobs structure with architecture and build_date columns. NATSSubmitter extension on API server enqueues enrichment_jobs rows on SBOMIngested events. Syft remains isolated to cmd/scanner-worker.

title: Artifact "SBOMs" tab is a Versions tab — paginate, label, and count by version
description:
## Problem

The "SBOMs" tab on the artifact detail page mislabels and miscounts what it shows, and its pagination is broken. Surfaced by the Traefik artifact, where the footer says "Showing 1-25 of 39" but only ~4 rows are visible.

These are two faces of the same problem: the tab is conceptually a Versions tab, but it is wired to a SBOM-paginated API and labelled with the raw SBOM count.

## Evidence

- API GET /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/sboms (internal/api/search.go:282) returns paginated raw SBOM rows. Query ListSBOMsByArtifact in db/queries/artifact.sql:50-69 uses COUNT(*) OVER() over raw SBOMs and ORDER BY s.created_at DESC LIMIT/OFFSET.
- Frontend SBOMsTab (web/src/pages/ArtifactDetail/SBOMsTab.tsx:21-24) calls groupSBOMsByVersionAndArch (web/src/utils/groupSBOMs.ts:8-27), keeping only the newest SBOM per (subject_version, architecture). For Traefik, 39 raw SBOMs collapse to 4 unique versions.
- Pagination (SBOMsTab.tsx:262-265) reflects the raw count (39), not the displayed row count.
- Paging "next" advances by raw-SBOM offset; older revisions of the same 4 versions can be returned, again collapsing — pagination does not actually reach distinct content.
- Tab button (ArtifactDetail/index.tsx:227-234) reads "SBOMs ({a().sbomCount})" — SBOM-flavored label and count for a version-flavored table.

## Root cause

Vocabulary drift. The page was built around versions but kept SBOM nomenclature and a SBOM-paginated API.

## Fix direction

Introduce GET /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/versions that: paginates by subject_version, rolls up architectures and newest revision per version server-side, returns a version count. Frontend: rename tab to Versions, switch to new endpoint, drop client-side grouping.

## Affected files

- internal/api/search.go
- internal/service/search_sbom.go
- db/queries/artifact.sql
- web/src/pages/ArtifactDetail/index.tsx
- web/src/pages/ArtifactDetail/SBOMsTab.tsx
- web/src/utils/groupSBOMs.ts
- web/src/api/queries/artifacts.ts
notes:
Files: db/queries/artifact.sql, internal/repository/artifact.sql.go (generated), internal/repository/repository.go, internal/service/search.go, internal/service/search_artifact.go, internal/api/types.go, internal/api/search.go, internal/api/router.go, web/src/api/client.ts, web/src/api/queries/artifacts.ts, web/src/api/queries/index.ts, web/src/pages/ArtifactDetail/VersionsTab.tsx (new), web/src/pages/ArtifactDetail/index.tsx
Approach: Added GET /api/v1/artifacts/{id}/versions with two-CTE SQL query (sboms_meta + DISTINCT ON for newest-per-version + array_agg for architectures). CountArtifactVersions feeds versionCount into ArtifactDetail. Frontend: replaced SBOMsTab with VersionsTab (server-side grouping, no client-side groupSBOMsByVersionAndArch), tab renamed to Versions (N). Deleted SBOMsTab.tsx and groupSBOMs.ts.
